title: Jayson Tatum looked like a star again as Celtics roll past Hornets: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum led the Celtics with 32 points, 8 assists, and no turnovers in a 114-99 win over the Hornets.
Payton Pritchard scored 28 points and had 6 assists, returning to the starting lineup for the Celtics.
Ron Harper Jr. scored 7 points before twisting his ankle, playing notable minutes for Boston.

title: Jaylen Brown boasts on social media after Celtics clinch playoff berth for 12th straight season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Celtics secured their 12th consecutive playoff berth after defeating the Charlotte Hornets 114-99.
Celtics' record improved to 50-24, reaching 50 wins for the fifth straight season despite key player absences.
Jaylen Brown celebrated the victory on social media, while Jayson Tatum led with a season-high 32 points.

title: Connelly Early continues to shine for Red Sox after strong first start in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Connelly Early pitched 5.1 innings with one run allowed in Red Sox's 3-2 loss to the Cincinnati Reds.
Early, 23, has a 2.19 ERA over five MLB starts, showcasing potential in Boston Red Sox's rotation.
Initially outside Boston's rotation, Early started due to Ranger Suarez's delay and Oviedo's spring struggles.

title: Boston Fleet cinch berth in Walter Cup Playoffs with win over Minnesota Frost
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Fleet clinched a Walter Cup Playoffs spot by defeating Minnesota Frost 4-2 at Grand Casino Arena.
The Fleet is the first team to secure a spot and fastest in history with a 13-5-2-4 record.
Rookie Haley Winn opened the scoring for Boston; Sophie Shirley scored twice, including a third-period insurance goal.

title: Woman charged in fatal South Boston stabbing
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Gisselle Pascual, 24, charged with manslaughter after a South Boston stabbing incident Friday, police report.
The victim succumbed to a stab wound a day after the altercation at 258 Old Colony Ave.
Pascual's initial charges were upgraded from assault to manslaughter; arraignment set in South Boston District Court.

title: Brockton police shoot man who allegedly fired at officers, man sleeping nearby also injured, DA says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Brockton police shot a 44-year-old man who allegedly fired at officers after a vehicle pursuit early Sunday.
A 71-year-old man nearby was grazed by a bullet during the gunfire exchange and received non-life threatening injuries.
Massachusetts State Police are investigating the incident, with a handgun found on the scene linked to the suspect.

title: Former Bruins coach Bruce Cassidy abruptly fired by Vegas before playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruce Cassidy was fired by the Vegas Golden Knights less than a month before the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs.
John Tortorella will replace Cassidy as head coach of the Golden Knights, aiming to improve their recent performance.
Vegas, third in the Pacific Division, has struggled with goalie depth, losing six of their last seven games.

title: New NFL mock draft has Patriots addressing major need on defense with 31st pick
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: New England Patriots projected to pick Clemson's T.J. Parker with the 31st pick in 2026 NFL Draft.
T.J. Parker recorded 21.5 sacks and six forced fumbles over three seasons at Clemson.
Patriots seeking to improve pass rush after recording only 35 sacks and losing K'Lavon Chaisson to free agency.

title: North Shore police department asking for public’s help after theft of historical plaques
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Newburyport police investigate theft of a historical plaque from Old Hill Cemetery's African American section on March 24.
Other similar bronze plaques have been stolen from Oak Hill Cemetery, Cashman Park, and Atkinson Common.
Police urge public assistance in locating stolen plaques, asking anyone with information to call 978-462-4411.

title: Bruins sign Merrimack goalie Max Lundgren to entry-level contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins signed Merrimack College goalie Max Lundgren to a one-year entry-level contract for the 2026-27 season.
Lundgren, 23, posted a 21-16-2 record with a 2.55 GAA and a .920 save percentage for Merrimack last season.
The Swedish goalie helped Merrimack win its first Hockey East title and won the tournament MVP award.
